evals.prompt: I want to set up my GitHub profile to look good for recruiters. My username is 'jchen-dev'. I'm a full-stack developer at a fintech startup, working mostly with React, Node.js, and PostgreSQL. I want to seem professional but approachable.
evals.expected_behavior: Recognizes this as profile-mode GitHub presence work, accounts for personal profile surfaces, and includes README, bio, and pinned-repository guidance tailored to recruiter-facing positioning.
assertions: Treats the task as GitHub profile work rather than repository-only work
Accounts for the personal profile README surface

evals.prompt: We're setting up our company's GitHub org. We need default community health files, issue templates, and a good org profile page in a .github repository.
evals.expected_behavior: Recognizes org-defaults mode, recommends the shared .github repository surfaces, and includes both templates and org profile guidance.
assertions: Distinguishes org-wide defaults from a single repository
Includes community health files and templates
Mentions the organization profile README surface

evals.prompt: I just open-sourced a Python CLI tool called 'taskflow'. The repo has a basic README and a LICENSE but nothing else. Can you make it look professional?
evals.expected_behavior: Handles this as repository-mode GitHub presence work, audits README and community-file gaps, and recommends repo-surface improvements tailored to a small open-source project.
assertions: Treats the task as repository presentation and health-file work
Identifies missing README-adjacent and community-health surfaces
Does not confuse the repo task with profile optimization
